page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6455B processor running at a high clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance characteristics across compute and memory subsystems. The instance achieves top-tier status in Geekbench single-core and multi-core metrics, as well as single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. Passmark database operations and LLM inference for medium and large prompt processing models also rank in the top tier. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks rank in the strong category.

Given its high-frequency Intel Xeon processor and dense DDR5 memory configuration, the r7iz.16xlarge offers a highly resource-dense profile for workloads requiring both high compute speeds and large memory footprints. The absence of local storage means storage-heavy workloads must rely on network-attached options, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive database operations, software compilation, real-time text and image processing, and CPU-based LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6455B processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory, delivering 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, single-threaded compression, and Passmark database operations. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in LLM inference for medium and large models. This configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, software compilation, and real-time data processing workloads that require high-frequency CPU performance alongside substantial memory capacity.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for databases and CPU-based LLM inference.

tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized instance featuring DDR5 memory and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for demanding database and compute workloads.

512.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
